rationale: Moe (2006) uses Bradlow and Rao (2000) for a specific modeling function, not just background. In developing Stage 1 search choice, it explicitly discusses Bradlow and Rao's dispersion-based assortment approach and then adapts that dispersion idea to a sequential clickstream setting. The cited work is tied to the paper's contribution on modeling interdependencies among viewed items, and it is referenced again when summarizing how the model was built.
relation_description: The citing paper applies and adapts Bradlow and Rao's assortment-dispersion modeling idea to represent dependence among sequential product-view choices in Stage 1. It transfers the dispersion concept from bundle-level assortment choice to a computationally feasible sequential two-stage clickstream model.
